description_long: This object is a stylized crossbow rifle with a robust, mechanical design, featuring a metallic frame and a wooden stock and grip. The limbs of the crossbow are curved and metallic, connected by a taut string, and the central body houses a cylindrical magazine with visible yellow slots, suggesting a repeating or multi-shot mechanism. The stock is angular and appears to be made of wood with a metallic buttplate, while the grip is ergonomically shaped for comfortable handling. The trigger and trigger guard are clearly defined, and there is a simple iron sight mounted on top of the receiver. The overall construction combines both modern and traditional elements, giving it a unique, hybrid appearance. The finish is mostly matte with some glossy highlights on the metal parts, and the color palette includes gray, black, brown, and yellow accents.
description: A hybrid crossbow rifle with a metallic frame, wooden stock and grip, and a cylindrical magazine with yellow slots. It features curved metal limbs, a taut string, and a simple iron sight.
description_view_0: Front view showing the curved metallic limbs, taut string, and the central cylindrical body with the grip visible below.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the wooden stock, ergonomic grip, cylindrical magazine with yellow slots, and the trigger assembly.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the stock's shape, the back of the receiver, and the alignment of the limbs and string.
description_view_3: Right side view displaying the magazine, stock, grip, and the mechanical details of the crossbow's body.
